info.title: AT Protocol XRPC API
info.summary: An unofficial conversion of AT Protocol's lexicons to OpenAPI's schema format.

get.summary: Get all sandboxes for a given actor

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The DID or handle of the actor

schema.description: The DID or handle of the actor

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of sandboxes to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of sandboxes to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: isRunning
parameters.description: Filter sandboxes by their running status.

schema.description: Filter sandboxes by their running status.

get.summary: Get the profile of an actor

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The DID or handle of the actor

schema.description: The DID or handle of the actor

token.description: An access token that can be used to authenticate with the terminal service. This token is typically short-lived and should be used immediately to establish a connection with the terminal.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the file to retrieve.

schema.description: The ID of the file to retrieve.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve files.

schema.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve files.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of files to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of files to skip before starting to collect the result set.

id.description: The ID of the file to delete

post.summary: Create a backup of a sandbox by id

description.description: An optional description for the backup.

post.summary: Create a new integration for the sandbox.

base.description: The base sandbox URI to clone from, e.g. a template or an existing sandbox.

provider.description: The provider to create the sandbox on, e.g. 'daytona', 'vercel', 'cloudflare', etc.

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

keepAlive.description: Prevent the sandbox from being automatically stop after a period of inactivity. Use with caution, as this may lead to increased costs.

spriteToken.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing sprite resources

redactedSpriteToken.description: A redacted token for accessing sprite resources

denoDeployToken.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing Deno Deploy resources

redactedDenoDeployToken.description: A redacted token for accessing Deno Deploy resources

daytonaApiKey.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing Daytona resources

redactedDaytonaApiKey.description: A redacted token for accessing Daytona resources

daytonaOrganizationId.description: The organization ID for Daytona resources

vercelApiToken.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing Vercel resources

redactedVercelApiToken.description: A redacted token for accessing Vercel resources

vercelProjectId.description: The project ID for Vercel resources

vercelTeamId.description: The team ID for Vercel resources

modalTokenId.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing Modal resources

redactedModalTokenId.description: A redacted token for accessing Modal resources

modalTokenSecret.description: A token (encrypted) for accessing Modal resources

redactedModalTokenSecret.description: A redacted token for accessing Modal resources

e2bApiKey.description: An API KEY (encrypted) for accessing E2B resources

redactedE2bApiKey.description: A redacted API KEY for accessing E2B resources

hopxApiKey.description: An API KEY (encrypted) for accessing hopx resources

redactedHopxApiKey.description: A redacted API KEY for accessing hopx resources

runloopApiKey.description: An API KEY (encrypted) for accessing Runloop resources

redactedRunloopApiKey.description: A redacted API KEY for accessing Runloop resources

post.summary: Execute a command in a sandbox.

command.description: The command to execute in the sandbox.

stdout.description: The output of the executed command.

stderr.description: The error output of the executed command, if any.

post.summary: Expose a port for a sandbox.

previewUrl.description: The preview URL for the exposed port.

post.summary: Expose an instance of VS Code for a sandbox. This allows users to access a web-based version of VS Code that is connected to their sandbox environment, enabling them to edit code, manage files, and perform other development tasks directly from their browser.

previewUrl.description: The preview URL for the exposed port.

get.summary: Get the list of backups for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

get.summary: Get the list of exposed ports for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

get.summary: Get the integrations for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID or URI to retrieve

schema.description: The sandbox ID or URI to retrieve

get.summary: Get a sandbox by ID or URI

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID or URI to retrieve

schema.description: The sandbox ID or URI to retrieve

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of sandboxes to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of sandboxes to skip before starting to collect the result set.

get.summary: Get the SSH keys for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

get.summary: Get the Tailscale token for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

get.summary: Get the Tailscale token for a sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The sandbox ID.

schema.description: The sandbox ID.

post.summary: Pull a directory from the cp storage into a Sandbox.

sandboxId.description: ID of the Sandbox into which to pull the directory.

uuid.description: ID of the directory in cp storage to pull into the Sandbox.

directoryPath.description: Destination path within the Sandbox where the directory should be pulled. This should be an absolute path.

post.summary: Push a directory from a Sandbox to the cp Storage.

sandboxId.description: ID of the source Sandbox to read the directory from.

directoryPath.description: Path of the directory within the Sandbox to push to cp storage. This should be an absolute path.

uuid.description: The UUID of the pushed directory in cp storage. This can be used to reference the directory in other API calls, such as creating a file from the directory or sharing the directory with another sandbox.

post.summary: Add or update SSH keys for a sandbox.
Store a Tailscale Auth Key for the sandbox. This Auth Key will be used to authenticate with the Tailscale API and manage the sandbox's Tailscale node.

authKey.description: The Tailscale Auth Key (encrypted) to store for the sandbox.

post.summary: Store a Tailscale token for the sandbox. This token will be used to authenticate with the Tailscale API and manage the sandbox's Tailscale node.

token.description: The Tailscale token (encrypted) to store for the sandbox.

post.summary: Restore a backup of a sandbox

repo.description: The git repository URL to clone into the sandbox before starting it. Optional.

keepAlive.description: Prevent the sandbox from being automatically stop after a period of inactivity. Use with caution, as this may lead to increased costs.

post.summary: Unexpose a port for a sandbox.

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the secret for which to retrieve secrets.

schema.description: The ID of the secret for which to retrieve secrets.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve secrets.

schema.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve secrets.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of secrets to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of secrets to skip before starting to collect the result set.

id.description: The ID of the secret to update.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve services.

schema.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve services.

parameters.description: The ID of the variable for which to retrieve variables.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve variables.

parameters.description: The ID of the volume for which to retrieve volumes.

schema.description: The ID of the volume for which to retrieve volumes.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: The ID of the sandbox for which to retrieve volumes.

The unique identifier of the actor.

displayName.description: The display name of the actor.

avatar.description: The URL of the actor's avatar image.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the actor was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the actor was last updated.

sandboxId.description: The ID of the sandbox to which the file belongs. This is used to associate the file with a specific sandbox environment.

path.description: The file path within the sandbox, e.g. '/app/config.json', '/home/user/.ssh/id_rsa', etc.

content.description: The content of the file. This will be written to the specified path within the sandbox. The content should be base64 encoded if it's binary data.

path.description: The file path within the sandbox, e.g. '/app/config.json', '/home/user/.ssh/id_rsa', etc.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the file was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the file was last updated.

io.pocketenv.port.defs.portView.description: A view of a port exposed by a sandbox.

previewUrl.description: A URL for previewing the service running on the port

key.description: The public key value, e.g. an SSH public key string.

provider.description: The provider of the sandbox, e.g. 'daytona', 'vercel', 'cloudflare', etc.

website.description: Any URI related to the sandbox

logo.description: URI to an image logo for the sandbox

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

provider.description: The provider of the sandbox, e.g. 'daytona', 'vercel', 'cloudflare', etc.

status.description: The current status of the sandbox, e.g. 'RUNNING', 'STOPPED', etc.

baseSandbox.description: The base sandbox that this sandbox was created from, if any. This can be used to determine the template or configuration used to create the sandbox.

website.description: Any URI related to the sandbox

logo.description: URI to an image logo for the sandbox

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

value.description: The value of the secret. This will be encrypted at rest and redacted in any API responses.

io.pocketenv.sandbox.defs.variablePref.description: A variable to add to the sandbox

value.description: The value of the variable. This will be visible in API responses and should not contain sensitive information.

io.pocketenv.sandbox.defs.filePref.description: A file to add to the sandbox

encrypt.description: Whether the file content should be encrypted at rest and redacted in API responses. This is useful for files that may contain sensitive information.

path.description: The path within the sandbox where the file will be created, e.g. '/app/config.json'. If not provided, the file will be created in the root directory of the sandbox.

io.pocketenv.sandbox.defs.volumePref.description: A volume to add to the sandbox

path.description: The mount path within the sandbox where the volume will be attached, e.g. '/data', '/logs', etc.

readOnly.description: Whether the volume should be mounted as read-only

name.description: The name of the sandbox provider

apiKey.description: The encrypted API key used to authenticate with the sandbox provider.

redactedApiKey.description: The redacted API key for the sandbox provider, returned in API responses.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization in the sandbox provider, if applicable. This can be used to associate the sandbox with a specific organization or team within the provider's platform.

vercelProjectId.description: The project ID for Vercel, if the sandbox provider is Vercel. This is used to determine which Vercel project the sandbox should be created in.

vercelTeamId.description: The team ID for Vercel, if the sandbox provider is Vercel and the sandbox should be created within a specific team. This is used to determine which team within the Vercel project the sandbox should be associated with.

modalTokenId.description: The token ID for Modal, if the sandbox provider is Modal. This is used to determine which Modal token to use when creating the sandbox.

modalTokenSecret.description: The token secret for Modal, if the sandbox provider is Modal. This is used to determine which Modal token secret to use when creating the sandbox.

redactedModalTokenId.description: The redacted token ID for Modal, returned in API responses when the sandbox provider is Modal. This can be used to identify which Modal token is being used without exposing the actual token ID.

redactedModalTokenSecret.description: The redacted token secret for Modal, returned in API responses when the sandbox provider is Modal. This can be used to identify which Modal token secret is being used without exposing the actual token secret.

e2bApiKey.description: The API KEY for E2B, if the sandbox provider is E2B. This is used to determine which E2B API KEY to use when creating the sandbox.

redactedE2bApiKey.description: The redacted API KEY for E2B, returned in API responses when the sandbox provider is E2B. This can be used to identify which E2B API KEY is being used without exposing the actual API KEY.

hopxApiKey.description: The API KEY for hopx, if the sandbox provider is hopx. This is used to determine which hopx API KEY to use when creating the sandbox.

redactedHopxApiKey.description: The redacted API KEY for hopx, returned in API responses when the sandbox provider is hopx. This can be used to identify which hopx API KEY is being used without exposing the actual API KEY.

runloopApiKey.description: The API KEY for Runloop, if the sandbox provider is Runloop. This is used to determine which Runloop API KEY to use when creating the sandbox.

redactedRunloopApiKey.description: The redacted API KEY for Runloop, returned in API responses when the sandbox provider is Runloop. This can be used to identify which Runloop API KEY is being used without exposing the actual API KEY.

id.description: Unique identifier of the SSH key.

privateKey.description: The private SSH key (redacted in API responses)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the SSH key was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the SSH key was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier of the Tailscale Auth Key.

authKey.description: The Tailscale auth key (redacted in API responses)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the Tailscale Auth Key was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the Tailscale Auth Key was last updated.

name.description: The name of the integration, e.g. 'GitHub', 'Slack', 'Trello', etc.

webhookUrl.description: The webhook URL of the integration.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the integration was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the integration was last updated.

directory.description: The directory that was backed up.

description.description: An optional description for the backup.

createdAt.description: datetime when the backup was created.

provider.description: The provider of the sandbox, e.g. 'daytona', 'vercel', 'cloudflare', etc.

website.description: Any URI related to the sandbox

logo.description: URI to an image logo for the sandbox

repo.description: A git repository URL to clone into the sandbox, e.g. a GitHub/Tangled repo.

readme.description: A URI to a README for the sandbox.

items.description: A path to be mounted as a volume in the sandbox, e.g. '/data', '/logs', etc.

Name of a secret to be added to the sandbox environment. Secrets are typically encrypted and stored securely, and can be used to store sensitive information such as API keys, database credentials, etc.
Name of an environment variable to be added to the sandbox environment.

name.description: Name of the secret, e.g. 'DATABASE_URL', 'SSH_KEY', etc.

sandboxId.description: The ID of the sandbox to which the secret belongs. This is used to associate the secret with a specific sandbox environment.

name.description: Name of the secret, e.g. 'DATABASE_URL', 'SSH_KEY', etc.

value.description: Value of the secret. This will be encrypted at rest and redacted in any API responses.

id.description: Unique identifier of the environment variable.

name.description: Name of the environment variable, e.g. 'NODE_ENV', 'PORT', etc.

value.description: Value of the environment variable. This will be visible in API responses and should not contain sensitive information.

sandboxId.description: The ID of the sandbox to which the environment variable belongs. This is used to associate the variable with a specific sandbox environment.

name.description: Name of the environment variable, e.g. 'NODE_ENV', 'PORT', etc.

value.description: Value of the environment variable. This will be visible in API responses and should not contain sensitive information.

name.description: Name of the volume, e.g. 'data-volume', 'logs', etc.

path.description: The path within the sandbox where the volume will be mounted, e.g. '/data', '/logs', etc.

readOnly.description: Whether the volume should be mounted as read-only within the sandbox. Defaults to false (read-write).

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the volume was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the volume was last updated.

sandboxId.description: The ID of the sandbox to which the volume belongs. This is used to associate the volume with a specific sandbox environment.

name.description: Name of the volume, e.g. 'data-volume', 'logs', etc.

path.description: The path within the sandbox where the volume will be mounted, e.g. '/data', '/logs', etc.

readOnly.description: Whether the volume should be mounted as read-only within the sandbox. Defaults to false (read-write).
